content_markdown: Which aspect of thermal physics is best explained by the molecular kinetic model?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present in the image.

options.label: A
options.text: The equation of state of ideal gases

options.label: B
options.text: The difference between Celsius and Kelvin temperature

options.label: C
options.text: The value of the Avogadro constant

vibe_explanation: The molecular kinetic model uses the motion and collisions of individual particles to derive macroscopic properties. By applying Newton's laws to these particles, we can derive the relationship between pressure, volume, and temperature, which is the basis for the equation of state of ideal gases ($PV = nRT$).
